joke: I told my computer I needed a break, and now it won’t stop sending me KitKats.
Why don’t skeletons fight each other? They don’t have the guts.
What do you call cheese that isn’t yours? Nacho cheese.
Why did the scarecrow win an award? Because he was outstanding in his field.
Why did the bicycle fall over? Because it was two-tired.
Why did the math book look so worried? It had too many problems.
Why did the musician bring a ladder? To reach the high notes.
Why did the golfer bring two pairs of pants? In case he got a hole in one.
Why was the calendar so popular? Because it had a lot of dates.
What do you call a bear with no teeth? A gummy bear.
Why was the broom late? It swept in.
What did one wall say to the other wall? I'll meet you at the corner.
Why did the tomato blush? Because it saw the salad dressing.
Why did the pencil get a job? It wanted to draw a steady income.
Why did the suitcase go to therapy? It had too much baggage.
Why did the pillow get promoted? It was always supportive.
Why did the belt get arrested? For holding up a pair of pants.
Why did the volcano break up with the earthquake? There was too much pressure.
Why did the photograph go to jail? Because it was framed.
What did the ocean say to the beach? Nothing, it just waved.
Why did the candle get invited to every party? Because it knew how to light up a room.
Why did the barber win the race? He knew a shortcut.
How do you organize a space party? You planet.
Why did the dollar bill take a class? To make more cents.
I was going to tell a joke about procrastination, but I'll do it later.
Why can’t you trust atoms? Because they make up everything.
I tried yoga once, but it was a real stretch.
Why did the dentist become a detective? He always got to the root of the problem.
I used to be addicted to the hokey pokey, but then I turned myself around.
Why did the teacher wear sunglasses? Because her students were so bright.
